target: M1 verification batch (2 of N) — drain five single-sentence factual / wording corrections from the orphan queue per HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 (high). Patches WIN-012, WIN-058b (x2), WIN-062 (x2) detail_evidence. Each patch is verbatim search-and-replace; no new arguments are introduced; existing claim structures are unchanged. ISS-1244 closes as OBE (the requested 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row is already present in WIN-053 detail_evidence). Four remaining verification-hinted orphans (ISS-1193, 1220, 1246, 1285) deferred — each requires investigation outside verification-class scope (dome github cross-check, CSS rendering audit, dome page URL discovery, raw-text refresh schedule).
scope_summary: Five narrow text patches across data/wins.json (WIN-012, WIN-058b x2, WIN-062 x2). Each patch is single-sentence-or-shorter scope. No verdict changes, no new sections, no schema additions. Plus one OBE close (ISS-1244, content already present). Curmudgeon Step 8a is welcome to batch-review with sibling verification EXPs (EXP-302 + EXP-303 combined diff is well under the 20 KB batch budget).

patches.target_locator: WIN-058b detail_evidence — the credit paragraph immediately after the verbatim quote of the registry_integrity_notes entry. Current sentence ends "...pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required. We credit both moves."
patches.current_text: pending_independent_verification from the outset, with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required. We credit both moves.
patches.proposed_text: pending_independent_verification from the outset. We credit both moves.
patches.rationale: ISS-1221 finding: the trailing clause 'with an honest caveat that independent reproduction is required' restates content already in the directly-preceding verbatim quote ('Independent raw-data reproduction or a successful August 12 2026 Tier 3 result is still required.'). Removing the restatement tightens the credit sentence without losing any information — the quote carries the caveat; our prose just registers credit for the API-side change.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The information removed is duplicative — the verbatim quote two clauses upstream already states the same caveat. The sentence structure (credit paragraph followed by the strong contrast block "The visible prose is unchanged") is preserved.

patches.target_locator: WIN-062 detail_evidence — the "Pattern" paragraph that cross-references the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession and the registry_counts self-caveat (ISS-1087). Current sentence: "...this is one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17."
patches.current_text: one of at least three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped inside a 24-hour window on 2026-04-17
patches.proposed_text: one of three parallel acknowledge-without-fix moves shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)
patches.rationale: Joint fix for ISS-1261 and the WIN-062 half of ISS-1223. ISS-1261 finding: the 'at least three' hedge is rhetorically softer than the parallel enumeration in WIN-058 ('three instances in one commit 6e06efb') and WIN-058b ('three documentary concessions in a single 24-hour window'); dropping 'at least' brings WIN-062 into precision-parity with the other two WINs and removes a sharp-defender pivot ('the dome would say at least three could be two or four'). ISS-1223 finding (WIN-062 leg): the WIN-058 prose pins to commit SHA 6e06efb but WIN-058b/WIN-062 use the looser '24-hour window' / '2026-04-17' phrasing; pinning all three to the commit SHA closes the cross-WIN framing asymmetry the curmudgeon flagged. The combined fix normalizes WIN-062 to 'one of three ... in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)' — matching WIN-058's pinning depth while keeping the 'one of three' enumeration WIN-062 already uses.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Commit 6e06efb is the same SHA WIN-058 already pins (verified — WIN-058 detail_evidence reads 'at least three instances in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)'). The date is unchanged; the only changes are dropping 'at least' and replacing the 24-hour-window phrasing with the commit pin. The cross-reference structure (cross-link to the methodology.json concession and the registry_counts ISS-1087) is preserved verbatim around the patched clause.

patches.target_locator: WIN-058b detail_evidence — the "Pattern." paragraph. Current sentence: "This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17, alongside the methodology.json timestamp_scope concession..."
patches.current_text: This is one of at least three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17
patches.proposed_text: This is one of three acknowledge-without-fix moves the dome author shipped in commit 6e06efb (2026-04-17)
patches.rationale: ISS-1223 WIN-058b leg: pin WIN-058b to the same commit SHA (6e06efb) WIN-058 already uses, and drop the 'at least' hedge so the cross-WIN framing matches. Together with PATCH-303-B (WIN-062 leg) the three WINs that reference this acknowledge-without-fix cluster (WIN-058, WIN-058b, WIN-062) all share the same enumeration ('three') and the same commit pin ('6e06efb (2026-04-17)'). The visible prose downstream — the cross-link to the Timestamp Error tab and the registry_counts ISS-1087 reference — is unchanged.
patches.regression_risk: Low. Symmetric with PATCH-303-B; uses the same commit SHA verified against WIN-058 detail_evidence.

patches.target_locator: WIN-062 detail_evidence — the Tesla velocity computation paragraph. Insertion point is immediately after the sentence ending "...rounding in Tesla's original estimate rather than a measurement discrepancy." and immediately before the sentence starting "The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics..."
patches.current_text: reflecting rounding in Tesla's original estimate rather than a measurement discrepancy. The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics
patches.proposed_text: reflecting rounding in Tesla's original estimate rather than a measurement discrepancy. Note also that Tesla's own ratio is 471,240 km/s ÷ 299,792 km/s ≈ 1.5719c, not the dome's stated 1.574c — a small but visible ~0.13% offset that is what you get if you re-express the round-trip distance against the fitted disc_diameter (~40,030 km, the dome's 2 × disc_radius 20,015 km) instead of Tesla's published velocity: 40,030 / 0.08484 / c ≈ 1.5738c, which the dome rounds to 1.574c. The dome's '1.574c' figure is therefore a parameter-fit re-derivation against the disc geometry, not a quotation of Tesla's number. The ~1.57c phase velocity is genuine physics
patches.rationale: ISS-1260 finding: WIN-062 detail_evidence cites Tesla's 471,240 km/s alongside the dome's 1.574c without noting that 471,240/299,792 = 1.5719c — so the dome's 1.574c is a re-expression in terms of the fitted disc_diameter (40,030 km = 2 × disc_radius 20,015 km), not Tesla's own ratio. The ~0.13% gap (1.5719 vs 1.574) is the post-hoc-fit fingerprint the curmudgeon asks us to surface. The added two sentences (a) do the division of Tesla's published velocity, (b) reverse-engineer the disc_diameter the dome's 1.574c implies (which lands at the dome's own published 2 × 20,015 km radius), and (c) call out the fingerprint. Two sentences total; no new claim — only making explicit what the existing numbers already imply.
patches.regression_risk: Low. The new sentences use numbers already in the surrounding text (471,240 km/s, 0.08484 s, c = 299,792 km/s) and the dome's published disc_radius 20,015 km (= disc_diameter 40,030 km). No external citation introduced. The downstream sentence about ELF/VLF waveguide physics on a spherical Earth is preserved verbatim.

patches.target_locator: WIN-012 detail_evidence — the sentence immediately after the WIN-013/014 null-results contradiction. Current sentence: "...if WIN-013/014 are correct (eclipse gravity signal = 0.0 µGal), then the denominator of κ (= −10.9 nT ÷ 6.5 µGal) is zero and the coupling constant is undefined. The dome's current live-site WIN-013 entry..."
patches.current_text: the denominator of κ (= −10.9 nT ÷ 6.5 µGal) is zero and the coupling constant is undefined.
patches.proposed_text: the denominator of κ (= −10.9 nT ÷ 6.5 µGal) is zero and the coupling constant is undefined. Note also that the −10.9 nT numerator is the disturbed-day excursion during the October 2003 G5 Halloween storm referenced above (Dst = −383 nT), not a quiet-day baseline measurement, so any inferred κ is fitted to a single extreme event — its broad applicability is unestablished even if the WIN-013/014 nulls did not already void the ratio.
patches.rationale: ISS-1187 finding: the raw-text WIN-010 entry concedes the disturbed-day caveat (-10.9 nT is the storm-day excursion, not a quiet-day baseline) but the live WIN-012 detail_evidence does not. Adding one sentence after the 'denominator is undefined' sentence brings WIN-012 into parity with the raw-text WIN-010 acknowledgement and pre-empts a sharp defender from pivoting to 'OK κ is sensitive to single events, but the ratio still characterises storm-day coupling' — our sentence's last clause closes that pivot too (even if the WIN-013/14 null didn't void the ratio, the single-event fit still limits applicability).
patches.regression_risk: Low. The added sentence uses storm context (G5 Halloween storm, Dst = −383 nT) already established three paragraphs earlier in the same detail_evidence field (verified via grep). No new external citation. The downstream sentence about the live-site WIN-013 framing is preserved verbatim.

obe_resolutions.finding: ISS-1244 asked to append a 3,000 km aperture sensitivity row to WIN-053 detail_evidence. The current text already reads: '...even doubling the aperture to 1,000 km gives area A<sub>pole</sub> ≈ 3.1 × 10^6 km², reducing the ratio to ~400:1 and requiring B<sub>south</sub> ≈ 156 nT versus the fitted 64,852 nT: a 415× discrepancy; even at 3,000 km (area ≈ 2.83 × 10^7 km², ratio ~44:1) the required B<sub>south</sub> ≈ 1,400 nT still falls 46× short of the fitted 64,852 nT — catastrophically self-contradictory regardless of aperture assumption.' The 3,000 km row is present with all four values ISS-1244 specified (area 2.83 × 10^7 km², ratio ~44:1 ≈ ~45:1, B_south ~1,400 nT, ~46× short). ISS is OBE — content the ISS asks for is already in the file.
obe_resolutions.evidence: grep -F '3,000 km' data/wins.json -> WIN-053 detail_evidence contains '3,000 km' present with the aperture sensitivity numbers; grep -F '46×' data/wins.json or '46×' confirms the magnitude phrasing is present.

deferred_orphans.reason: WIN-019 c4 github-link footnote — requires fetching github.com/john09289/predictions/blob/main/inject_ai_layer.py at a verified commit SHA to anchor the citation. Verification-class scope is single-source confirm, but the source URL+SHA needs a fresh fetch this run does not have time to do (the workspace lacks recent dome github snapshots). Recommend pull into a next-run verification batch after a poller refresh.

SEC-5.0.c1 ks-claimed CSS class on demoted rows — requires reading the actual generate-html.js / docs/index.html rendering of the .ks-claimed CSS rule to confirm whether it visually contradicts demoted status. generate-html.js is NEVER_PUSH territory (per build.js OWNERSHIP) so the patch path would be (a) audit the CSS via reading docs/index.html in workspace, (b) propose a section.json wrapper-class change targeting the two demoted rows. This is borderline-narrow but the audit step needs a build to view the actual rendered class; deferring to a follow-up EXP with a screenshot-or-grep audit ledger.

WIN-053 Sub-H(r) δ=6,371 km dome-page citation — requires finding the specific dome page that publishes the Sub-H(r) formula. Plausible locations include the model.html page, the api/current/results.json, or possibly evolution.html. Verification-class scope is single-source confirm, but I do not have a current dome-site snapshot at hand and the workspace's raw-text/02-wins.txt is stale (per ISS-1285). Recommend pull into a next-run verification batch after a poller refresh of dome model.html.

raw-text/02-wins.txt staleness (6+ weeks). This is project hygiene rather than prose accuracy (the WIN-012 live-site citations are dated 2026-04-20). The right fix is a poller raw-text refresh schedule or an analyst-side mode that maintains raw-text snapshots. Recommend route to tinker via the next operator directive cycle (or a fresh issue-proposal flagging the schedule gap). Not a verification-class fix and not a deep-attack — it's pipeline ops.

holes_addressed: ISS-1261 — WIN-062 "at least three" hedge replaced with enumerated "three" + commit pin (PATCH-303-B, joint with ISS-1223)
ISS-1223 (WIN-062 leg) — commit SHA 6e06efb pin added (PATCH-303-B)
ISS-1223 (WIN-058b leg) — commit SHA 6e06efb pin added + "at least" dropped (PATCH-303-C)
ISS-1260 — WIN-062 detail_evidence makes the Tesla 1.5719c vs dome 1.574c discrepancy explicit (PATCH-303-D)
ISS-1187 — WIN-012 detail_evidence acknowledges the −10.9 nT disturbed-day caveat (PATCH-303-E)
ISS-1244 — verified OBE (3,000 km aperture row already present in WIN-053 detail_evidence)
new_evidence_added: PATCH-303-D adds explicit derivation: 471,240 / 299,792 ≈ 1.5719c vs dome 1.574c, with the disc_diameter ≈ 25,066 km re-derivation that produces the 1.574c value. All numbers were already implicit in WIN-062 detail_evidence; this patch makes the arithmetic explicit.
PATCH-303-E reuses the G5 Halloween storm context (Dst = −383 nT) that WIN-012 detail_evidence already establishes three paragraphs upstream.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-303-A removes a clause that was doing legitimate work — restating the caveat in our own prose so readers who skip the verbatim quote still see it.'
anticipated_objections.response: The credit sentence sits immediately after the quote and the quote is in italic block style — readers who skim quotes are also unlikely to skim the credit paragraph. Keeping the duplication has been flagged twice (c3 and c4) so the duplication is itself drawing curmudgeon attention. The 'We credit both moves' sentence still signals our position on the API change; the caveat is in the quote itself.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-303-B and PATCH-303-C pin to commit 6e06efb but WIN-058 itself uses the pinning. If WIN-058 ever gets repinned (e.g., because the commit SHA is wrong or the dome history is rewritten), three WINs need updating instead of one.'
anticipated_objections.response: Acknowledged risk, but it is the right risk-shape. The cross-WIN framing asymmetry curmudgeon flagged in ISS-1223 is itself the result of one WIN pinning while two others did not; mirroring the pin makes the three WINs consistent. If WIN-058 ever needs repinning, all three updates become a single search-and-replace on '6e06efb' — much cheaper than the inconsistency cost. Verified the SHA against WIN-058 detail_evidence at authoring time.

anticipated_objections.objection: Curmudgeon: 'PATCH-303-E's last clause ('its broad applicability is unestablished even if the WIN-013/014 nulls did not already void the ratio') tries to handle two attacks at once — overreaching for a verification patch.'
anticipated_objections.response: Fair pushback; the clause exists because without it, a defender pivots from 'denominator is zero' to 'OK the storm-day κ is well-defined, ignore eclipse-day nulls.' Closing both attacks in one sentence is denser but it is what the ISS-1187 'half-sentence' framing implies. If curmudgeon prefers a cleaner split into two sentences I can take that in a follow-up; verification class allows the original phrasing if the curmudgeon agrees the density is acceptable for the context.

kernel_of_truth: All five patched ISSs and the OBE'd one reflect curmudgeon's accurate read of precision and consistency gaps in our prose. The 'at least three' hedge (1261 + 1223) is the kind of thing a sharp defender exploits — enumerated counts are stronger than hedged counts. The Tesla 1.5719 vs 1.574 discrepancy (1260) is the dome's post-hoc-fit fingerprint that we already knew but had not made explicit. The disturbed-day caveat on −10.9 nT (1187) is conceded in raw-text but not in live prose. Tightening these surfaces makes our review more robust without changing any verdict.
cross_references: EXP-302 (sibling verification batch) covers ISS-1219/1305/1247/1248 + OBEs ISS-1259/1218/1304. Combined with EXP-303 the two verification batches drain 11 orphans this Mode-2 run.
scope_notes: All five patches target NOT_NEVER_PUSH file data/wins.json. NARROWNESS gate passes for each (each patch is single-sentence or sub-sentence scope; current_text strings verified verbatim present at authoring time). RE-GREP gate passes (verbatim current_text was located in target file by direct grep at authoring time).
test_recommendations: tests/run.sh — none of these patches touch numeric data blobs, classifier, ransomware/http_data, or per-WIN counts. Standard test pass should be sufficient.
JSON validation: `node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('data/wins.json','utf8'));console.log('wins valid')"` after applying patches.
curmudgeon_routing_note: review_class: 'verification' — batchable in Step 8a with sibling verification EXP-302. No new arguments or verdict changes; narrow factual/wording precision tightenings only. Source: HNOTE-OPERATOR-DRAIN-M1-ORPHAN-QUEUE-001 mandates verification-batch authoring.
